The fact that there is no checking account due to the absence of a checking account independently contributes a protective signal, because the regression results explicitly indicate a lower estimated risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that there is no known savings account due to standard credit risk models typically rely on financial ratios, income accounts, and balance sheet data, because the regression results explicitly indicate a lower estimated risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that the loan duration is extended due to extended repayment periods inherently prolong the lender's exposure to borrower uncertainty, because the empirical findings explicitly state that longer durations increase the likelihood of credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [3]
